title: Target repository command authority
summary: Target-repository verification MUST use commands and toolchain entrypoints verified from that repository rather than Pancreator language or package-manager assumptions.
instructions: Agents MUST treat runtime/repository-checks.json as the scoped deterministic verification profile for the active workspace and MUST NOT substitute guessed npm, Python, Rust, Java, or other ecosystem commands.

Repository-check commands MUST be copied from or directly derived from authoritative target documentation, manifests, executable scripts, or operator instructions; uncertainty MUST leave a profile unconfigured rather than inventing a command.

Commands that depend on a particular interpreter, virtual environment, package manager, compiler, SDK, or runtime MUST use the repository-declared explicit entrypoint when available instead of an ambiguous PATH alias.
Each profile SHOULD include probes that record the executable identity and version whenever different toolchain selections could make results incomparable across implementation, review, and QA.

Implementation, review, and QA MUST use the same configured profile commands for equivalent checks. Missing profiles or commands MUST be reported as not configured and MUST NOT be represented as passing validation.

Embedded installations MUST remain language- and technology-agnostic. Pancreator self-development policies, handbooks, scripts, and package-manager conventions MUST NOT be imposed on a target repository unless the target independently adopts them.
